channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: As Tesla ​reports smallest profit since pandemic, company plans to shift Fremont factory to production of Optimus robots
item.description: CEO Elon Musk says the company will close down production of two older car models, S and X, and convert the Fremont factory to produce its Optimus robots instead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Tiny autonomous robots can now swim on their own
item.description: Scientists create smallest programmable robots ever – smaller than salt grains, these breakthrough microscopic machines could revolutionize medicine and healthcare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EVs just outsold gas cars in Europe for the first time
item.description: EVs hit a new milestone: In December, buyers in Europe registered more electric cars than gas cars for the first time. EV registrations hit 217,898 in the EU last month—up 50% year-over-year from 2024. Sales of gas cars, on the other hand, dropped nearly 20% to 216,492.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: Napoli vs Chelsea Free Streams: How to watch Champions League 2025/26 online from anywhere
item.description: All the ways to watch Napoli vs Chelsea in the 2025/26 Champions League with both sides in desperate need of three points.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: Roku just made a major tweak to its home screen – and not everyone is happy about it
item.description: A new Subscriptions tab has appeared on the Roku home screen, letting you pick up where you left off.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: Valve is facing a lawsuit over 'monopoly' claims on Steam – and I'm scratching my head at this one
item.description: Valve's Steam storefront is favoured by a large majority of PC gamers, with generous discounts and pricing, but a lawsuit has called that into question.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Spanish soccer league offers reward of 50 euros for tips to ID bars illegally broadcasting games
item.description: The Spanish league says it is stepping up its fight against audio-visual fraud by offering 50 euros for each verified tip on the establishments broadcasting games without proper permission.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Amazon confirms 16,000 job cuts, including to AWS
item.description: Amazon is cutting about 16,000 jobs across the company, SVP of People Experience and Technology Beth Galetti wrote in an email to employees Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 11 cool things Copilot can do in Excel
item.description: Microsoft has woven its generative AI technology throughout Microsoft 365, the company’s productivity suite. Its Copilot AI assistant is most often used in M365 apps for text-oriented actions, such as generating email or document drafts in Outlook or Word, or writing summaries of Teams meetings.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The UK government gets it spectacularly wrong on AI – just 3% of the public agree with its stance on copyright law changes
item.description: A major public consultation revealed overwhelming opposition to the UK government's AI copyright proposal
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: It's not Dante's Inferno 2, but this upcoming indie game takes inspiration from the same classic poem and could be the sequel to Visceral's 2010 game I've been hoping for all these years
item.description: Upcoming indie game The Alighieri Circle Dante's Bloodline looks like an intriguing psychological thriller, and taking its inspiration from the classic poem, The Divine Comedy, has its hooks in me already.
item.dc:creator: Rob Dwiar, TechRadar

item.title: TikTok settles youth social media addiction lawsuit ahead of trial
item.description: TikTok agreed to settle a landmark social media addiction lawsuit just before the trial kicked off, the plaintiff’s attorneys confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: This company pioneered virtual therapy for OCD. Now it wants to do the same for PTSD
item.description: When Stephen Smith started NOCD 11 years ago, he wanted to build an app for people like himself—one of the 8.2 million Americans with obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD)—to track their symptoms and time their therapy exercises.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Barker, Fast Company

item.title: Gemini Flash model gets visual reasoning capability
item.description: Google has added an Agentic Vision capability to its Gemini 3 Flash model, which the company said combines visual reasoning with code execution to ground answers in visual evidence. The capability fundamentally changes how AI models process images, according to Google.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Who needs 5G, or even 6G? New breakthrough can transmit at 15 gigabytes per second - that's enough for a whole weekend's worth of movies
item.description: UC Irvine researchers developed a 140GHz transceiver achieving 15 GB/s, overcoming DAC limits while reducing power consumption and simplifying production.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: This new privacy-focused phone service is designed to keep your phone from getting hacked
item.description: A privacy-centric cellphone carrier called Cape is now officially available across the United States, offering a unique set of features to protect users from surveillance and identity theft.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok, YouTube, and Meta are headed to court for a landmark trial over social media addiction
item.description: Three of the world’s biggest tech companies face a landmark trial in Los Angeles starting this week over claims that their platforms—Meta’s Instagram, ByteDance’s TikTok, and Google’s YouTube—deliberately addict and harm children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic cofounder Daniela Amodei says trusted enterprise AI will transcend the hype cycle
item.description: While Silicon Valley argues over bubbles, benchmarks, and who has the smartest model, Anthropic has been focused on solving problems that rarely generate hype but ultimately determine adoption: whether AI can be trusted to operate inside the world’s most sensitive systems.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: How AI-induced cultural stagnation is already happening
item.description: Generative AI was trained on centuries of art and writing produced by humans. But scientists and critics have wondered what would happen once AI became widely adopted and started training on its outputs. A new study points to some answers.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: In Davos, warnings about AI and jobs
item.description: The impact of AI on the labor market is more serious than previously expected, with entry-level jobs collapsing and white-collar jobs in danger, participants at the World Economic Forum (WEF) said last week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Grubhub confirms data breach amid extortion claims
item.description: Grubhub data breach confirmed as sources say ShinyHunters group allegedly demands Bitcoin payment to prevent release of stolen customer support data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2026 network outage report and internet health check
item.description: ThousandEyes, a Cisco company, monitors how ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services are handling any performance challenges and provides Network World with a weekly roundup of events that impact service delivery.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Anthropic integrates third‑party apps into Claude, reshaping enterprise AI workflows
item.description: Anthropic has added a new capability inside its generative AI-based chatbot Claude that will allow users to directly access applications inside the Claude interface.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Uber unveils a new robotaxi with no driver behind the wheel
item.description: Uber robotaxi testing begins on public roads in San Francisco Bay Area with plans to launch driverless rides in a major U.S. city later in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI robot brings emotional care to pets
item.description: Aura AI pet robot tracks behavior changes to detect when pets feel excited, anxious or relaxed. Tuya Smart's device offers laser play, treat dispensing and voice interaction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Photonic chip vendor snags Gates investment
item.description: Neurophos has landed another $110 million in funding from investors including Gates Frontier, Bill Gates’ venture capital outfit, to move its photonic chips nearer to production. The growing use of AI has led to increasing demands on computational power within data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World
